title: Add scan-pipeline Sentry spans and measurements
acceptance_criteria: scan-worker.ts wraps ALL scan work (including setMeasurement) inside Sentry.startSpan({ name: 'scan', op: 'scan.run' }, async (span) => { ... }). The callback returns the awaited scan result — span does not close early.

The root span is tagged with attributes: scan_id (the scan UUID), tier (user tier string). Raw URL is NOT an attribute — only url_hash (first 8 hex chars of a sha256 digest of the URL, using Node crypto, no new dependency).
Sentry.setMeasurement('scan.duration_ms', elapsedMs, 'millisecond') is called inside the span callback after runScanInternal resolves (on success, timeout, and error paths).

Two child spans are added in src/lib/scanner/index.ts: one wrapping the Playwright/static-fetch crawl phase, one wrapping LLM enrichment if it runs. Scanner module groups (group1/group2) do NOT get their own spans — too granular.
On all scan outcomes (complete, timeout, error) logger.info('scan_complete', { scanId, durationMs, result: 'complete'|'timeout'|'error', tier }) is called.
When Sentry is not initialized (no DSN), Sentry.startSpan and setMeasurement are no-ops — confirm this does not throw or change behaviour.
npm run typecheck passes (startSpan generics must satisfy TypeScript strict mode).
npm run test passes — no regressions in existing scan-worker tests.
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
